hypothesis: Adding an explicit round-trip cycle-consistency loss across paired molecular representations (e.g., SMILES→SELFIES→SMILES and SMILES→InChI→SMILES) during STR-Bamba pretraining will produce statistically significant gains in cross-representation translation fidelity (validity, Tanimoto/BLEU) and downstream property prediction performance on MoleculeNet and polymer benchmarks compared to the current two-phase training without cycle constraints.
